source_jobs.title: Head of Business Operations @ Waniwani
source_jobs.description:
WANIWANI


NEW YORK - CAN BE STARTED IN PARIS.




Waniwani is building the AI distribution infrastructure for the $10T services economy. We enable service vendor to sell quote-based services (insurance, financing, wealth, software and more) directly inside LLMs like ChatGPT, where buying decisions are moving, and built the Agent to Agent commerce future.

Our first product generated its own type of SaaSpocalypse. We launched the world's first insurance app unlocking quotes in ChatGPT. It triggered $79B market crash for the world's largest brokers. The press called it "the industry's ChatGPT moment." Now thousands of companies want to deploy their own AI-native storefronts through our infrastructure.

We are global from day one: US, Australia, Middle East, Korea, Europe. Our team is small, the ambition is not.




THE ROLE



Clients hire Waniwani because we deliver. This role ensures delivery stays world-class as we scale.

You will own the delivery function, the operating cadence, and the systems and agentic workflows that make execution predictable. You will set standards, create leverage, and build the team over time.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Client outcomes: scope control, timelines, quality bar, stakeholder management, and executive communication. Clear accountability from kickoff to sign-off.

 - Delivery leadership: run the portfolio, allocate resources, and make the hard trade-offs. Escalate early, decide fast, and keep work moving.

 - Customer-facing operating cadence: weekly health reporting, milestones, risk tracking, and crisp written updates that clients trust.

 - The internal OS: SOPs, templates, QA checklists, dashboards, and handoffs that make delivery repeatable and auditable.

 - Agentic operations: automation, internal copilots, and reusable assets that remove manual work and compound across projects.

 - Build the function: hire and lead a small ops and delivery team as volume grows; define roles, rituals, and performance expectations.




YOU

 - You’ve led client delivery in an agency, consultancy, or high-velocity startup.

 - You operate like an owner: structured thinking, strong judgment, and relentless follow-through.

 - You communicate clearly with senior stakeholders and can hold a firm line on scope and priorities.

 - You build systems that get adopted, then evolve them with real feedback.

 - You can hire, coach, and set a high bar.

source_jobs.title: Founding Growth Lead @ Waniwani
source_jobs.description:
WANIWANI


NEW YORK - CAN BE STARTED IN PARIS.

Waniwani is building the AI distribution infrastructure for the $10T services economy. We enable service vendor to sell quote-based services (insurance, financing, wealth, software and more) directly inside LLMs like ChatGPT, where buying decisions are moving, and built the Agent to Agent commerce future.

Our first product generated its own type of SaaSpocalypse. We launched the world's first insurance app unlocking quotes in ChatGPT. It triggered $79B market crash for the world's largest brokers. The press called it "the industry's ChatGPT moment." Now thousands of companies want to deploy their own AI-native storefronts through our infrastructure.

We are global from day one: US, Australia, Middle East, Korea, Europe. Our team is small, the ambition is not.




THE ROLE

First growth hire.

Our first marketing initiative was a press release that worked too well, triggering an 80B$ insurance market crash in the public markets. It put AI distribution and Waniwani on the agenda of every financial institution’s board and kick-started an intense growth motion.

We’ve signed partnerships with the most renowned industry players and delivered several keynotes on AI distribution to Fortune 500 executives. But luck is not a playbook. As founding marketing & GTM, your role is to build Waniwani’s repeatable, scalable growth engine from scratch: events, partnerships, outbound, and content. You’ll identify which channels convert, then scale them.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Build the Growth Engine: You won’t be following a playbook; you’ll be writing it. You’ll hire and lead a team across different regions to figure out how we win in each market.

 - Be the Face on the Ground: Find the 5% of events and dinners that actually matter. Build the relationships that turn into a multi-million dollar pipeline.

 - Drive the Global Narrative: Write the stuff people actually want to read. Market deep-dives, case studies, and sharp commentary that proves we are the experts.

 - Secure Tier-1 Partnerships: Identify the organizations that already have our customers' attention. Structure the deals and manage them until they produce revenue.

 - Scale through Data: If it doesn’t move the needle, kill it. Use the numbers to decide where we hire next and which markets we double down on.
   
   


YOU:

 - Startup Veteran: You’ve built revenue from scratch before, likely when the company had zero brand recognition and a tiny budget.

 - Sharp Writer: You can write a 3-sentence email that gets a reply from a CEO and a 1,000-word article that gets shared by industry leaders.

 - High EQ: You know how to work a room and how to follow up without being a nuisance.

 - Leader & Maker: You’re comfortable managing a team across time zones but aren't afraid to get your hands dirty and book the first 50 meetings yourself.

 - Creative Problem Solver: You find the "side doors" into big accounts when the front door is locked.

source_jobs.title: Founding Sales @Panora (backed by Hexa)
source_jobs.description:
À PROPOS DE PANORA

Panora développe une suite d'agents IA pour les courtiers en assurance : un secteur hautement réglementé et opérationnellement complexe.

Notre ambition : devenir l'OS IA des courtiers en Europe, en automatisant les workflows à forte friction tout en améliorant la conformité, la qualité du conseil et les relations clients.

Le produit est déjà en production chez des cabinets de courtage en France et en Belgique, avec des assistants IA opérationnels (automatisation des cotations, comparaison de contrats, analyse des couvertures, vérification de conformité, génération de documents…).

Panora permet aux courtiers d'économiser des heures chaque semaine, de réduire le temps d'analyse jusqu'à 70%, et de se concentrer sur ce qui compte vraiment : conseiller leurs clients.

Fondée par Diane, ex-VC avec une solide expérience opérationnelle, et Fabian, fondateur récidiviste et ingénieur IA orienté produit, Panora est soutenue par Hexa (anciennement eFounders), le studio de startups à l'origine d'Aircall, Spendesk et Front.

Nous entrons dans une phase clé de scaling et d'industrialisation de notre plateforme IA.

Nous recherchons des builders qui souhaitent travailler à l'intersection des opérations d'assurance, du produit et de l'IA.




POURQUOI CE RÔLE EXISTE

Panora a atteint le product-market pull.

Le produit est déjà déployé chez des cabinets de courtage, générant des gains opérationnels mesurables et résolvant des workflows douloureux dans un secteur longtemps sous-servi par le logiciel.

Nous entrons maintenant dans un nouveau chapitre : scaler l'exécution commerciale, construire un moteur go-to-market reproductible, et devenir la plateforme IA de référence pour les courtiers en Europe.

C'est pourquoi nous recrutons notre premier Founding Sales.

En tant que Founding Sales de Panora, vous travaillerez directement avec Diane https://www.linkedin.com/in/diane-rdb/ (CEO) et jouerez un rôle clé dans la façon dont l'entreprise se développe. Vous serez responsable de la génération de revenus, structurerez notre motion commerciale et contribuerez à transformer une forte demande marché en une machine commerciale scalable.

C'est un rôle de leadership opérationnel pour quelqu'un qui souhaite à la fois closer des deals stratégiques et poser les bases de la croissance future.




VOS RESPONSABILITÉS


CROISSANCE DES REVENUS & EXÉCUTION COMMERCIALE

 - Posséder et exécuter la stratégie commerciale de Panora : acquisition, conversion et expansion

 - Closer des clients stratégiques et construire des relations de confiance avec les cabinets de courtage et leurs parties prenantes senior

 - Affiner le positionnement, les messages et les narratifs commerciaux pour maximiser la conversion et l'adoption du produit

 - Contribuer à définir notre ICP et prioriser les segments clients les plus pertinents


GO-TO-MARKET & OPÉRATIONS COMMERCIALES

 - Construire et optimiser des processus de vente scalables, de la génération de pipeline au closing

 - Structurer notre CRM, reporting, supports commerciaux et playbooks

 - Améliorer l'outbound, les partenariats et les canaux d'acquisition pour générer une croissance prévisible

 - Itérer en continu sur la façon dont Panora génère et convertit son pipeline


COLLABORATION PRODUIT & INSIGHTS CLIENTS

 - Travailler étroitement avec les courtiers pour comprendre en profondeur leurs workflows, leurs points de douleur opérationnels et leurs processus de décision

 - Remonter les feedbacks clients, les objections et les réalités terrain dans les conversations produit

 - Collaborer étroitement avec Diane et Fabian pour orienter les priorités produit et améliorer la valeur utilisateur

 - Contribuer à positionner Panora comme un partenaire IA mission-critical dans un environnement réglementé


CONSTRUCTION D'ÉQUIPE & LEADERSHIP

 - Aider à recruter, onboarder et mentorer la future équipe Sales

 - Définir ce qu'une excellente exécution commerciale signifie chez Panora

 - Construire une cul

source_jobs.title: Lead Data Engineer @Panora (backed by Hexa)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PANORA

Panora is building a suite of AI-powered agents for insurance brokers - a highly regulated and operationally complex industry.

Our ambition is to become the AI Operating System for brokers in Europe: automating high-friction workflows while improving compliance, advisory quality, and client relationships.

The product is already live with brokerage firms across France and Belgium, with AI assistants in production (quotation automation, contract comparison, coverage analysis, compliance checks, document generation…).

Panora enables brokers to save hours every week, reduce analysis time by up to 70%, and focus on what truly matters: advising clients .

Founded by Diane du Paty (ex-VC, operator) and Fabian Langlet (repeat founder, AI product engineer), Panora is backed by Hexa (Aircall, Spendesk, Front).

We are entering a key phase of scaling and industrializing our AI platform.

At Panora, AI is not a feature - it is the core product layer, deployed in real-world conditions with strong constraints on reliability, traceability, and compliance.




ROLE

We’re looking for a Founding Data Engineer to help build and scale the systems powering Panora’s AI products.

Your work will sit at the intersection of:

 - AI systems: LLMs, agentic workflows, evaluation pipelines, tracing and observability

 - Data engineering: ingestion, normalization, enrichment, extraction and document-processing pipelines

 - Internal datasets: building high-quality, structured insurance datasets from policies, quotes, underwriting questionnaires and business rules

 - Product & backend engineering: designing robust APIs, data models and scalable services used directly in production

 - Insurance expertise: translating real-world insurance workflows, contract language and decision rules into usable data systems

As the third engineering hire, you'll work directly with Fabian (CTO & Co-founder) and Jeremy (Founding Software Engineer), with strong ownership and direct impact on both product and technical direction.



What to Expect

 - Join an existing, large-scale codebase and quickly develop a deep understanding of the systems powering Panora.

 - Improve, refactor and scale critical parts of the platform while contributing new capabilities where they create the most impact.

 - You'll work with complex workflows, unstructured data and real production constraints.

 - You'll own problems end-to-end, from AI systems and data pipelines to customer impact.

 - Success is measured by product impact, reliability and customer outcomes.
   
   

Responsibilities:


BUILD & IMPROVE AI PRODUCTS

 - Design, ship and improve AI-powered workflows used daily by insurance brokers

 - Build evaluation, feedback and monitoring systems to continuously improve performance

 - Turn complex insurance workflows into reliable AI-powered products


BUILD THE DATA FOUNDATIONS

 - Build and maintain data pipelines powering our products

 - Process and structure unstructured data (contracts, emails, insurer documents)

 - Improve the quality, reliability and observability of our systems


OWN & SCALE SYSTEMS

 - Own systems end-to-end: from design and implementation to deployment and monitoring

 - Contribute to architecture and key technical decisions

 - Help define how AI, data and engineering scale at Panora
   
   


OUR TECHNOLOGY STACK

Built for an AI-first, production-grade product:

 - Languages: Python for AI, data and automation; TypeScript for backend services and product integrations

 - AI systems: LLM-powered agents, structured extraction, evaluation frameworks, tracing, observability and feedback loops

 - Infrastructure: AWS, with a serverless and managed-services approach designed for reliability and scale

 - Data: MongoDB, document-processing pipelines, structured extraction workflows, internal datasets and evaluation datasets

 - Integrations: Microsoft 365, CRM and ERP tools, insurer extranets and other systems used daily by insurance brokers

source_jobs.title: Founding Sales @Plato (backed by Hexa)
source_jobs.description:
⚖️ WHO ARE WE



Plato is building the OS for litigation lawyers.

With GenAI, the legal industry is on the verge of a major transformation. Litigation lawyers are overwhelmed by complex and repetitive tasks: complex damages calculations, thousands of documents to review, days spent drafting legal documents, and more.



Our mission: Build the leading OS for litigation lawyers, enabling lawyers to spend 10x less time on administrative and operational work, and focus instead on strategy.



Plato already supports around 15 law firms and 1 major insurance’s legal team specialized in personal injury, who use our solution daily to manage hundreds of active cases.

Founded in January 2026 by Ben https://www.linkedin.com/in/ben-lasseri/ (CEO) and Alexandre https://www.linkedin.com/in/alexandredeboutray/ (CTO), we are backed by Hexa (formerly eFounders), Europe’s leading startup studio. Hexa has launched over 35 companies, including unicorns such as Front, Aircall, and Spendesk, collectively generating more than €230M in ARR and raising over €750M in funding.

Plato is already live with real users and real data, solving concrete legal workflows in production. We’re now entering a key building phase and looking for someone ready to take real ownership and help shape the product, go-to-market strategy, and sales culture from day one.




🎯 YOUR MISSION



We're looking for our Founding Sales to work hand-in-hand with Ben, Co-founder & CEO.

You'll own the entire sales cycle, bringing Plato's AI platform to litigation law firms across France.

This is a high-impact execution role. You'll build pipeline, close deals, and work closely with the founders to continuously improve our messaging, sales playbook and product.

Your objective during your first six months will be to close around 30 new law firms while helping us build a repeatable commercial engine.



Responsibilities

 - Own the full sales cycle: From outbound prospecting to closing, you'll take ownership of every step of the sales process.

 - Build pipeline: Generate opportunities through outbound and qualified inbound leads.

 - Close deals: Sell directly to partners and decision-makers in litigation law firms.

 - Improve our GTM: Work closely with the founders to continuously refine our messaging, positioning and sales playbook.

 - Shape the product: Bring structured customer feedback to help prioritize product improvements.
   
   


🤩 THIS ROLE IS FOR YOU IF

You’re an experienced, ambitious sales professional who enjoys building processes from scratch and owning the revenue pipeline end-to-end.

You like working close to founders, making pragmatic strategic decisions, and closing deals in a fast-paced environment.



You’ll thrive in this role if you:

 - Have a successful experience closing B2B SaaS deals, ideally selling complex software to demanding customers, closing B2B SaaS deals, ideally in legal tech or complex enterprise software.

 - Native French speaker with excellent written and verbal communication skills.

 - Are comfortable owning a full sales cycle, from outbound prospecting to closing.

 - Have a strong business mindset: you care about understanding the client's core problems and demonstrating ROI.

 - Are pragmatic in execution: you know how to navigate complex organizations to find the decision maker.

 - Are energized by early-stage ambiguity and taking ownership alongside technical founders.

 - Bonus points if you have developed a strong understanding of the French legal ecosystem or you have an existing network in the legal industry (lawyers, law firms, insurers).
   
   


💎 WHAT WE OFFER

 - Ownership & Impact: As the first sales hire, you'll have real influence on the company's growth and GTM direction.

 - Sell a high-value AI product: Bring a solution to market that solves real, painful problems for lawyers.

 - Hexa ecosystem: Access to expertise, resources, and network from one of Europe's leading startup studios.

 - Performance-driven com

source_jobs.title: VP Finance and Investments
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HEXA

Hexa (formerly eFounders) gives entrepreneurs the best chances to build outstanding companies. We're a pioneering, world-leading startup studio born in Europe, with a track record comparable to tier-1 VC funds: 60+ companies created (including 3 unicorns), among them Aircall, Spendesk, Front, Swan and Yousign.

We're entering a special chapter. After 15 years building companies from scratch, Hexa is scaling: expanding our Ventures activity across Europe (Hexa Start and Hexa Sprint, taking entrepreneurs from zero to one) and growing Hexa Scale, our PE program that takes majority stakes in existing startups and pushes them to new thresholds.

We're hiring a VP FInance and Investments to structure the financing strategy of the holding company and play a central role in Scale's deal-making. It's a StratCo seat internally and a key point of contact for our investors externally, at a moment when the shape of Hexa for the next years is being decided.





THE MISSION

You'll be a strategic partner to the CEO and the Hexa Partners, sitting at the intersection of finance and deal-making strategy. You'll drive the financing of the holding company, own the relationship with our shareholders, and bring analytical firepower to Scale's investment decisions (including a seat on the board of several portfolio companies).

Think Swiss Army knife: equally at home in a financial model, a deal structuring conversation, an incentive mechanism, or a banking negotiation.





WHAT YOU'LL OWN



I/ Financing & holding strategy

 - Define and execute the financing strategy at the holding level (debt, equity, hybrid instruments)

 - Manage the shareholder relationship: cap table structuring, capital operations, reporting

 - Manage banking relationships and the group's financial partners

 - Steer treasury and capital allocation across the three tracks

 - Build and maintain the group's reference financial models

 - Manage a small team handling Hexa FP&A, operational LP management and regulatory matters

II/ Deal-making & investments (Scale)

 - Key contributor to Hexa Scale's investment decisions: due diligence analysis, BP, valuation, modeling

 - Challenge the structure of deals (majority acquisitions, term sheets, governance mechanisms)

 - Design incentive schemes (management packages, carried interest on the investment-team side, leaver provisions)

 - Sit on the board of a few portfolio companies
   
   



ABOUT YOU

 - You've spent 6-8 years in finance, including at least 3-4 years in investment banking (M&A, leveraged finance) or in an investment fund (investment team / deal team).

 - You're just as comfortable building a financial model as you are structuring a deal, designing incentives, or negotiating with a bank.

 - You're analytical and a fast learner, able to quickly get up to speed on new and heterogeneous topics.

 - You thrive in a fast-paced, low-structure environment.

 - You take full ownership and think strategically, rather than waiting for a playbook.

 - You've spent enough time in the tech and startup ecosystem to know how it works and speak its language.

 - You're bilingual French / English.

 - You're based in Paris.

source_jobs.title: Become a Tech Founder
source_jobs.description:
Ready to roll up your sleeves and become an ambitious Tech Founder to build the next big thing?

As a Tech founder at Hexa, you will have complete ownership & leadership on everything related to tech. But your impact will go far beyond tech.

We expect you to take part in strategic decisions (GTM strategy, fundraising matters etc) in the same way as the Business Founder.

 

Being part of Hexa means joining a strong & powerful tech community!

You will constantly be surrounded by tech peers (i.e Tech Founders from current batch but also from our portfolio companies https://www.hexa.com/companies), a huge opportunity for you to learn from inspiring CTOs with whom you’ll share knowledge with.

 

Last but not least, recruitment experts from Core Team will help you hire the best tech talents to build a strong tech team from the ground up - thanks to Hexa’s large network & powerful employer brand.

In just 12 months, you will go faster than you ever been: starting with an idea we want to build together to a fully autonomous company. We'll be at your side, helping you build your team, develop your product, secure your first clients, and raise funding—all while financially backing you until your seed round.

 

Are we the right fit for each other?

This article https://www.hexa.com/blog/article/faq-what-do-we-look-for-in-founders breaks down what makes a great Hexa founder, and our approach https://www.hexa.com/journey shows how we help founders go from idea to scale.

 

If you're building a company—whether now or in the future—let’s talk.

The right conversation at the right time can change everything.

Tell us about yourself [here] and let’s explore what we can build together 🚀

source_jobs.title: Sales Executive (full cycle) - SMB
source_jobs.description:
🚀 Filiz est une start-up innovante de la EdTech, en pleine croissance et dans un contexte international à venir, qui simplifie et automatise les démarches administratives et financières liées aux contrats d’alternance et de stage. Grâce à notre plateforme 100% en ligne (SaaS), nous facilitons la vie des écoles, des entreprises et des étudiants en rendant les processus plus fluides, rapides et collaboratifs. Fini la paperasse, finis les allers-retours interminables : tout se fait en quelques clics, de manière flexible, automatisée et centralisée.

 

⚡️ Filiz est accompagnée par Hexa Scale, une nouvelle initiative lancée par Hexa (ex-eFounders) - le startup studio à l’origine de Aircall, Spendesk, et Front.

Hexa Scale accompagne des entreprises SaaS B2B ambitieuses ayant de la traction, pour les aider à passer à l’échelle supérieure grâce à des investissements et un accompagnement opérationnel sur mesure.

 

💥 Filiz a déjà doublé son ARR deux années de suite, en s’appuyant presque exclusivement sur l’inbound et le bouche-à-oreille.

La machine Marketing tourne. L'équipe Sales se structure. C'est le bon moment pour rejoindre et performer.

 

🎯 Ta mission

Tu rejoins une équipe Sales naissante de 2 personnes : Elsa, notre Head of Sales et Eva, Sales full cycle, dans un contexte où les bases sont posées et les leads arrivent. Ton rôle : faire tourner un cycle de vente complet sur du SMB, avec vélocité et précision dans un marché qui répond.

Pas question de tout construire de zéro. Il s'agit d'exécuter, itérer et performer sur de l'inbound bien qualifié et de l'outbound que tu pilotes pour compléter ton pipe.

 

💯 Responsabilités

1. Gérer ton pipe de A à Z

 - Traiter les leads entrants générés par le marketing avec réactivité et méthode

 - Construire et alimenter ta partie outbound (environ 2/3 de ton pipe) : identification, qualification, approche des écoles cibles

 - Piloter tes opportunités avec rigueur, du premier contact à la signature

2. Closer avec empathie et efficacité

 - Mener les discovery et démos, gérer les objections, conclure sur des cycles courts, avec cadence

 - Maintenir une haute vélocité sans sacrifier la qualité de la relation

 - Travailler en proximité avec la Head of Sales pour affiner les approches et partager les bonnes pratiques

3. Développer le revenu au-delà du closing

 - Identifier et activer les opportunités d'upsell au sein de ton portefeuille client (nouveaux modules, montée en gamme)

 - Collaborer avec le marketing et le Customer Success pour maximiser l'expansion revenue

4. Contribuer à la structuration commerciale

 - Documenter les pitchs gagnants, les objections récurrentes, les séquences qui fonctionnent

 - Participer à l'enrichissement du Sales Playbook dans une équipe qui se structure sans repartir de zéro



🤩 Profil recherché

 - 3 à 6 ans d'expérience en ventes B2B SaaS, sur des cycles complets (prospection → closing)

 - Tu es à l'aise sur du SMB : tu sais gérer le volume, maintenir la cadence et closer sans t'éterniser

 - Tu es autonome et organisé·e, capable de jongler entre inbound et outbound sans perdre le fil

 - Tu closes avec méthode et empathie, sans bullshit, sans pression inutile

 - Tu maîtrises les outils modernes (CRM, automatisation, stack outbound)

 - Tu aimes évoluer dans un environnement en structuration, sans avoir besoin que tout soit déjà parfait

Bonus appréciés :

 - Une expérience sur des comptes grands groupes ou ETI en complément du SMB

 - Une expérience de vente sur un autre marché européen (Belgique, Espagne, Italie…)

 - Une connaissance du secteur EdTech ou des CFA / organismes de formation



🗺️ Poste basé à Paris, en télétravail partiel, dans une équipe majoritairement Full Remote.



⚙️ Process

 - Appel d’introduction avec l’équipe Talent (30min)

 - Entretien métier avec Elsa - Head of Revenue (1h)

 - Entretien avec Maxime - Founder & CEO (45min)

 - Étude de cas (1h)

 - Prises de références

source_jobs.title: Acoustic Researcher
source_jobs.description:
Full-time · On-site (Paris Marseille Amsterdam)

The sea is the physical layer of the economy—the conduit for our energy, communications, and trade. It is also Europe’s exposed flank. Pelagon is building the answer.

Founded by Aymeric https://www.linkedin.com/in/adepontbriand/?locale=en (serial deeptech entrepreneur; founded & exited Scortex, Leading AI solution for manufacturing quality) and Gauthier https://www.linkedin.com/in/gauthier-bouxin/?locale=en (Navy officer and McKinsey), Pelagon is building a foundational multimodal acoustic model for autonomous maritime protection (underwater and surface) in service of European sovereignty. It will be deployed on a distributed, autonomous network of vectors (UUVs, buoys, sailbuoys, seabed…) to provide real-time understanding of underwater threats.

France and Europe have world-class acoustic engineers and a long history in underwater warfare. By bridging this expertise with modern physical-AI strategies (as seen in autonomous driving and robotics). Europe is still missing it’s naval NeoPrime, Pelagon aims to become to lead the way in autonomous marine protection—from detection to interception—partnering with leading navies, primes, and neo-primes, while also operating its own fleet as a service.

Pelagon is backed by Hexa http://hexa.cc/, the pioneer startup studio in Europe, which has launched 35+ companies, including unicorns like Front, Aircall, and Spendesk, combining €230m+ ARR and €750m raised.

 

Why this matters

The sea is the physical layer of the economy — and Europe's most exposed flank. Everything that moves beneath the surface leaves an acoustic trace, but the ocean is a brutal channel: multipath, ambient noise, shifting propagation. Reading it is first and foremost a physics problem.

France and Europe have a world-class heritage in underwater acoustics and sonar. Pelagon's bet is to bridge that science with modern physical-AI strategies and a distributed fleet of UUVs, buoys, sailbuoys and seabed sensors. As our Acoustic Researcher, you are the physics backbone of that system — the person who understands underwater sound deeply enough that our models can learn from it. You partner directly with our AI Engineers, who consume the features, representations and data you help define.

What you'll do

 - Model underwater sound propagation across realistic ocean environments to enable our synthetic data generation pipeline.

 - Characterize and classify acoustic signatures of surface and underwater targets; build the ground-truth understanding our multimodal acoustic model is trained against.

 - Design and reason about sensor and array geometries: what to deploy, where, and how to extract maximum information from constrained & distributed vectors.

 - Work hand in hand with AI Engineers on physics-constrained learning strategies ans models.

What we look for

 - Deep grounding in underwater acoustics, sonar, or a closely related field (ocean acoustics, radar/array processing, acoustic signal processing). PhD or equivalent research experience.

 - Strong command of statistical signal processing: detection & estimation theory, array processing, time-frequency analysis.

 - Fluent in Python (NumPy/SciPy) for modeling, simulation and analysis; comfortable working with large datasets.

 - A builder's instinct, you want your work deployed at sea, not left in a paper. Bias toward validating ideas against real data.

 - Bonus: prior experience using machine learning on acoustic signals, naval / defense domain experience.

 

Why join now

 - Founding ownership. You'll be one of the first engineers and will define the technical foundation of the company. Meaningful equity comes with that.

 - A mission with weight. This is hardware that protects critical infrastructure and Europe's maritime space.

 - Real things, in the real ocean, fast. You'll see your work in the water within weeks, not years.

 - AI-first, at the edge, in the real world. We train our own models and run the

source_jobs.title: Founding Product Designer @Panora (backed by Hexa)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PANORA

Panora is building a suite of AI-powered agents for insurance brokers: a highly regulated and operationally complex industry.

Our ambition: become the AI Operating System for brokers in Europe, automating high-friction workflows while improving compliance, advisory quality, and client relationships.

The product is already live with brokerage firms across France and Belgium, with AI assistants in production (quotation automation, contract comparison, coverage analysis, compliance checks, document generation…).

Panora enables brokers to save hours every week, reduce analysis time by up to 70%, and focus on what truly matters: advising their clients.

Founded by Diane, ex-VC with a strong operational background, and Fabian, repeat founder and product-driven AI engineer, Panora is backed by Hexa (formerly eFounders), the startup studio behind Aircall, Spendesk, and Front.

We are entering a key phase of scaling and industrializing our AI platform.

We're now looking for builders who want to work at the intersection of insurance operations, product, and AI.




WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

Insurance is complex, operational, and full of nuance: it cannot be designed through abstraction alone.

As our Product Designer, you will work directly with brokers, founders, and AI systems to help translate real-world insurance workflows into interfaces that actually work in practice.

This role is not for you if you're looking for a traditional design role focused on pixel-pushing or visual polish alone.

You will help shape how insurance knowledge is structured, displayed, and used inside an AI-native product.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Insurance discovery & user research

 - Work closely with our broker Design Partners to deeply understand their workflows

 - Learn how brokers reason about risks, pricing, coverage decisions, and compliance

 - Identify edge cases, exceptions, and non-standard situations that impact the user experience

Product design & delivery

 - Contribute to overall strategy and decision-making about product direction

 - Create, collaborate, and iterate on flows, prototypes, and high-fidelity visuals

 - Design and ship high-quality product improvements

 - Translate complex insurance logic into clear, actionable, and reliable interfaces

AI experience & prompt collaboration

 - Help design how AI outputs are presented, understood, and acted upon by brokers

 - Work closely with product and engineering to improve output quality, relevance, and usability

 - Contribute to refining how insurance nuance is reflected in the product experience




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We're looking for someone who thrives in complexity and wants to help build an AI-native product in a highly operational industry.

 - You're a doer.

 - You have experience designing UX and UI for software products

 - You are expert using Figma as your primary design tool

 - Experience working independently to explore concepts from beginning to end

 - Demonstrated ability to design simple solutions to complex user problems

 - An eye for high-quality, well-crafted visual and interaction design, and a passion for UI details

 - Proficiency in prototyping your ideas to articulate nuanced design decisions

 - You're an explorer and don't mind getting out of your comfort zone

 - You are comfortable with feedback and committed to continually exploring alternative solutions

 - You don't need to be an expert on insurance, but you need to be motivated by the mission
   
   


WHY JOIN

 - Founding-level responsibility early in your career

 - Direct exposure to product, engineering, and market participants (brokers, carriers)

 - Steep learning curve at the intersection of insurance, product, and AI

 - Real influence on how the product is built from day one

 - Competitive compensation package, including equity

 - Work from Hexa's offices alongside founders, operators, and other early-stage startups
   
   

Panora is committed to creating a diverse envir

source_jobs.title: AI Applied Researcher
source_jobs.description:
Full-time · On-site (Paris Marseille Amsterdam)

The sea is the physical layer of the economy—the conduit for our energy, communications, and trade. It is also Europe’s exposed flank. Pelagon is building the answer.

Founded by Aymeric https://www.linkedin.com/in/adepontbriand/?locale=en (serial deeptech entrepreneur; founded & exited Scortex, Leading AI solution for manufacturing quality) and Gauthier https://www.linkedin.com/in/gauthier-bouxin/?locale=en (Navy officer and McKinsey), Pelagon is building a foundational multimodal acoustic model for autonomous maritime protection (underwater and surface) in service of European sovereignty. It will be deployed on a distributed, autonomous network of vectors (UUVs, buoys, sailbuoys, seabed…) to provide real-time understanding of underwater threats.

France and Europe have world-class acoustic engineers and a long history in underwater warfare. By bridging this expertise with modern physical-AI strategies (as seen in autonomous driving and robotics). Europe is still missing it’s naval NeoPrime, Pelagon aims to become to lead the way in autonomous marine protection—from detection to interception—partnering with leading navies, primes, and neo-primes, while also operating its own fleet as a service.

Pelagon is backed by Hexa http://hexa.cc/, the pioneer startup studio in Europe, which has launched 35+ companies, including unicorns like Front, Aircall, and Spendesk, combining €230m+ ARR and €750m raised.



Why this matters

The sea is the physical layer of the economy — and Europe's most exposed flank. Submarines, drones and threats move through it unseen because the ocean is opaque by default. Pelagon exists to change that: to give Europe a real-time, sovereign understanding of what moves beneath the surface, from detection to interception.

At the core of that mission is a maritime foundational perception model — Pelagon's equivalent of the perception stack that made autonomous driving possible, but for the underwater domain. As an AI Engineer, you own the models at the heart of it, from research to deployment on a distributed, autonomous fleet of UUVs, buoys, sailbuoys and seabed sensors.



What you'll do

 - Design, train and ship state-of-the-art multimodal and acoustic models for underwater detection, classification and localization.

 - Build the training pipeline end to end: self-supervised pre-training on large unlabeled acoustic corpora, then fine-tuning for specific threats.

 - Fuse acoustic, kinematic and other sensor modalities into a single representation that holds up in low-SNR, real-world conditions.

 - Shape Pelagon's data strategy — how we capture, curate and compound proprietary data from our own fleet into a durable advantage.

 - Distill and optimize models to run on the edge: compute- and power-constrained hardware aboard autonomous vectors at sea.

 - Close the loop with sea trials — turn field data and failure cases into the next iteration.



What we look for

 - Strong track record training models deployed in production, ideally on audio, signal, time-series or other non-image modalities.

 - A physical-AI mindset: you've worked where models meet messy sensor data and real hardware, not just clean benchmarks.

 - Instinct for data strategy — you treat the data flywheel as a core part of the model, not an afterthought.

 - Confortable across the ML stack: data, architecture, training, evaluation and deployment. Bias for shipping; you measure success by what works at sea.

 - Bonus: background in signal processing, or experience in defense, maritime, robotics or autonomous driving.



Why join now

 - Founding ownership. You'll be one of the first engineers and will define the technical foundation of the company. Meaningful equity comes with that.

 - A mission with weight. This is hardware that protects critical infrastructure and Europe's maritime space.

 - Real things, in the real ocean, fast. You'll see your work in the water within weeks, not years

source_jobs.title: Internships
source_jobs.description:
Join Hexa as an Intern. Build the next generation of startups!

Hexa started as eFounders in 2011 and has since become a leading startup studio, giving entrepreneurs the best chance to build outstanding companies. We have built over 40 companies, including Aircall, Spendesk, Front, Swan, and Yousign, with a combined valuation of $4.5bn http://4.5bn.

At Hexa, interns don't fetch coffee. They shape companies. ✨

Whether you join our Core Team or one of our portfolio startups, you'll be embedded in real ventures, working alongside founders from day one.

If you're entrepreneurial by nature and want to learn more in 6 months than most people do in 3 years, keep reading.

 

Open internship opportunities

We are currently looking for interns across several teams:

- Scale Team - VC Analyst Support the Scale team in identifying, evaluating, and activating growth opportunities across Hexa's portfolio. You'll work closely with founders on go-to-market, partnerships, and expansion with a VC analyst mindset.

- Sprint Team - VC Analyst Join the Sprint team at the earliest stage of startup creation. You'll run market analyses, and work hands-on at the early phase of new ventures.

- Talent Team - Ops

At Hexa, finding the right people is a growth problem. You'll own the full sourcing stack and directly shape how we recruit founders and early employees across our portfolio.

- Studio - GTM Associate Work directly with the studio team on go-to-market strategy, business development, and growth for newly launched startups. Expect a mix of strategic thinking and hands-on execution.

- Portfolio Startups - Business Associate Join one of Hexa's freshly launched startups as its first business hire. You'll contribute across sales, ops, and strategy - and get to watch (and shape) a company being built from scratch.

 

What we look for in all our interns

- You don't need to have done it before. But you need to want to build. Across all these roles, we're looking for:

- An entrepreneurial mindset: you're full of ideas, you like to test things yourself, and you thrive in environments built on trust and autonomy

- Strong communication skills and intellectual curiosity: you're good at building relationships with people, you ask good questions and you're always curious about how things work!

- A genuine interest in startups, technology, and building things that matter.

 

Why intern at Hexa?

Work alongside repeat entrepreneurs and world-class operators Get real ownership and responsibility from week one Access to Hexa's network of founders, investors, and ecosystem leaders Based at La Cristallerie in Paris: with a gym, podcast studio, nap room, and vibrant startup community A caring, diverse, and impact-driven environment

 

Interested?

We'd love to hear from you. 😍 Send us an application telling us which role excites you most and why, and what you'd bring to the team. No lengthy cover letter needed: just be yourself.

 

Hexa is committed to creating a diverse environment. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment irrespective of gender, origins, identity, background and sexual orientation

source_jobs.title: Engineer in residence
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Hexa is building the infrastructure for exceptional entrepreneurs, wherever they are in their journey.

Our founding activity is a startup studio (Hexa Start): we create companies from scratch with outstanding founders, and support them intensively from idea to product-market fit. But we've grown beyond that. Hexa Sprint supports early-stage startups that already exist and need to accelerate hard. Hexa Scale works with more mature companies ready to go to the next level. Three distinct models, one shared ambition: back the right people and help them build something that lasts.

Earlier this year, we brought Tanguy Goretti https://www.linkedin.com/in/tanguygo/ on board as our CTO, a signal of where we're headed. He operates as a tech leader and ecosystem builder: supporting founders across our portfolio on technical decisions, pushing Hexa's employer brand in the engineering world, building partnerships with the best tools and labs out there, and helping early-stage CTOs level up. He's running a high-leverage function, and he's looking for someone to join him.



The role

You'll work directly alongside Tanguy and you're here to build!



What you'll actually do

 - Build internal tools that give our founders and core team an edge, on fundraising, recruiting, GTM. Launch agents that automate what slows us down.

 - Launch MVP for new startups alongside our partners, on the hottest topics, before there's a team. Short deadlines, fast shipping, real stakes. Probably the most exciting part of the job.

 - Support the Hexa tech ecosystem: our conferences with top labs, our CTO lunches with partners like Cloudflare or Vercel, and the community we're building around it.
   

What we're looking for

 - Recently graduated from a top engineering school

 - Deeply autonomous: you don't need hand-holding, you need a problem worth solving

 - Side projects that prove it. Cool ones.

 - AI-native: you use it in everything you touch, and it shows in what you've built in the last 6 months

 - TypeScript and/or Python.

 - Based in Paris (at least 3 days/week)
   

What's in it for you

 - Package: €72K to €85K gross/year (base salary of €60K–€71K + 20% annual profit sharing)

 - Opportunity to invest in Hexa portfolio startups at a (very) preferential rate

 - Swile meal card

 - 8 to 12 RTT days per year

 - Flexible remote work policy

 - Mobility budget: €500/year for green transportation (bike, metro, electric scooter)

 - Health insurance with 50% coverage for you and your family

 - 1% Holiday bonus

source_jobs.title: Forward-Deployed AI Builder
source_jobs.description:
🎯 THE ROLE IN ONE LINE

Hexa Scale's forward-deployed AI builder — the person who turns the firm's and the portfolio's AI ambitions into shipped agents, working workflows, and team-wide AI fluency.



🧭 WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

The Managing Partner and Operating Partner own investment selection, value-creation plans, and the strategic transformation conversations with portfolio CEOs. What we are missing is a dedicated builder and enabler who:

 - Ships the internal AI tooling that makes Hexa Scale move faster than any other PE firm in Europe.

 - Embeds with portfolio companies to actually build the first agents and AI workflows — not just advise on them.

 - Is hands-on enough to teach the deal team, the operating team, and portfolio operators how to use AI in their daily work.

Think Forward-Deployed Engineer with an AI specialty and the maturity to operate independently in front of executives — but without yet owning IC-level strategic calls.



🔑 KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


1. BE HEXA SCALE'S IN-HOUSE AI BUILDER (INTERNAL ENABLEMENT)

 - Build and maintain internal agents and scripts that compress repetitive work for the deal, operating, and finance teams.

 - Own the rollout of our internal AI stack (LLM tools, agent frameworks, knowledge base, evals).

 - Run regular "AI clinics" and short workshops to lift daily AI fluency across the team.


2. PRODUCTIONIZE THE AI LAYER OF OUR INVESTMENT PLATFORM

Working under the direction of the MP and OP, build and ship:

 - Market intelligence agents tracking signals across European vertical SaaS sub-segments.

 - Sourcing & pipeline agents that enrich and prioritize targets.

 - DD support tools: data-room ingestion, customer-call analysis, KPI extraction, AI-readiness checks.

 - Reporting automation for portfolio monitoring and LP communications.


3. SUPPORT INVESTMENT EVALUATION (ASSIST, DON'T LEAD)

 - Provide the technical AI lens during DD: tech & product audit, AI-readiness scoring, data-asset evaluation, agentic opportunity sketches.

 - Surface findings as structured inputs to the deal lead and the IC memo — strategic decisions stay with the MP / OP.


4. FORWARD-DEPLOYED AI ENGINEER FOR PORTFOLIO COMPANIES

 - Embed with portfolio companies in short sprints (typically 2–8 weeks) to build the first AI use cases: ship the first agent, integrate the orchestration layer, set up evals, get something into production.

 - Train internal counterparts so the work scales after the sprint ends.

 - Capture reusable components and playbooks for the next portfolio company.

 - This means regular travel to portfolio companies across France and Europe — expect to be on-site for a significant part of your time.
   


👤 WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4–6 years of experience as an applied AI/ML engineer, Forward-Deployed Engineer, founding engineer, or applied AI consultant.

 - Has shipped multiple LLM-powered or agentic products in production — comfortable with LLM tooling, RAG, evals, and integrating into messy enterprise codebases and data.

 - Builder mindset and high tempo: loves shipping, ships fast, iterates faster.

 - Comfortable parachuting into an unfamiliar codebase, vertical, or company and getting traction in days, not months.

 - Strong communicator; can teach AI to non-technical executives and operators.

 - Business-literate — cares about ROI and adoption, not just elegance — but not aspiring to be a deal partner.

 - English fluent; French is a strong plus.

 - Genuinely excited about travel — this role isn't remote-first. You'll be on the ground with portfolio teams across France and Europe on a regular basis.
   
   


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Background in vertical SaaS, B2B SMB software, or developer tools.

 - Experience designing agent orchestration, RAG over messy enterprise data, or AI evals/observability.

 - Prior FDE or applied AI consulting role with regular C-level exposure.

 - Comfort working across multiple European geographies and languages.
   


✅ WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LI

source_jobs.title: Become a Business Founder
source_jobs.description:
At Hexa, we don’t just launch startups —we co-found them with ambitious entrepreneurs who want to build world-changing software companies.

With over 45 companies launched, including Aircall, Spendesk, and Front, and a combined valuation approaching $5bn, we’ve built a proven track record in SaaS, fintech, AI, and beyond. Now, we’re expanding into new fields like climate, healthtech, and edtech—and we’re looking for founders ready to take on these challenges.

 

In 12 months, go further, faster

Building a startup is a long journey, but the first year is critical. At Hexa, you’ll move faster than ever—transforming an idea into a fully independent company in just 12 months.

- You won’t do it alone. Our team of 30+ experts works hand in hand with you across product, strategy, hiring, and go-to-market.

- You’ll leverage our track record. With decades of experience launching category-defining companies, we’ve developed a playbook to de-risk and accelerate the process.

- You'll have access to a powerful network. From first hires to first customers to first investors, our ecosystem gives you an unfair advantage.

- We’ll back you financially. We provide the financial support you need to get your company off the ground, ensuring you're set for success from day one.

 

Are we the right fit for each other?

We partner with founders who are both bold and grounded—those who dream big, execute fast, and stay humble along the way. As we like to say: Eyes on the stars, feet on the ground.

A deep passion for software and technology is just as crucial. It’s not only what we build—it’s what drives us forward.

 

Want to know if we’re the right match? This article https://www.hexa.com/blog/article/faq-what-do-we-look-for-in-founders breaks down what makes a great Hexa founder, and our approach https://www.hexa.com/journey shows how we help founders go from idea to scale.

 

If you're building a company—whether now or in the future—let’s talk.

The right conversation at the right time can change everything. Tell us about yourself here and let’s explore what we can build together 🚀

source_jobs.title: Embedded System Lead Engineer
source_jobs.description:
PELAGON - AUTONOMOUS MARITIME ISR

Full-time · On-site (Marseille or Paris) · Founding team · Reports to founders

The sea is the physical layer of the economy—the conduit for our energy, communications, and trade. It is also Europe’s exposed flank. Pelagon is building the answer.

Founded by Aymeric https://www.linkedin.com/in/adepontbriand/?locale=en (serial deeptech entrepreneur; founded & exited Scortex, Leading AI solution for manufacturing quality) and Gauthier https://www.linkedin.com/in/gauthier-bouxin/?locale=en (Navy officer and McKinsey), Pelagon is building a foundational multimodal acoustic model for autonomous maritime protection (underwater and surface) in service of European sovereignty. It will be deployed on a distributed, autonomous network of vectors (UUVs, buoys, sailbuoys, seabed…) to provide real-time understanding of underwater threats.

France and Europe have world-class acoustic engineers and a long history in underwater warfare. By bridging this expertise with modern physical-AI strategies (as seen in autonomous driving and robotics). Europe is still missing it’s naval NeoPrime, Pelagon aims to become to lead the way in autonomous marine protection—from detection to interception—partnering with leading navies, primes, and neo-primes, while also operating its own fleet as a service.

Pelagon is backed by Hexa http://hexa.cc/, the pioneer startup studio in Europe, which has launched 35+ companies, including unicorns like Front, Aircall, and Spendesk, combining €230m+ ARR and €750m raised.



THE ROLE

As Embedded System Lead Engineer, you take end-to-end technical ownership of the hardware, electronics, and embedded stack across a small family of autonomous maritime platforms, some operating on the surface, some beneath it.

What they share matters more than what sets them apart: each must persist at sea for long stretches, sense its surroundings, run our AI on-device, and report back intelligently — all while surviving a brutal environment on a very tight energy budget.

You will lead their technical development, from architecture and component selection through bring-up, integration, sea trials, and iteration. You'll set the engineering bar for the platforms team and shape how we build for years to come.

These platforms exist to carry one thing into the water: our AI. Pelagon trains high-performance detection models in-house on its own stack, and those models have to run on-device, at the edge, on a razor-thin power budget — then push results back to our operations center. Designing the compute-and-power architecture that makes this possible is the heart of the role.

The mandate is simple and uncompromising: get reliable hardware in the water fast, and start collecting data. Every week of persistent at-sea operation compounds. The hardware is the gate to all of it.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Common system architecture across the platforms: power, sensing, compute, and communications.

 - The edge-AI compute platform — selecting and integrating the processors and accelerators that run our in-house detection models on-device, and extracting maximum inference from every milliwatt.

 - Electronics design and integration — power distribution, battery systems, motor/thruster control, sensor interfaces (acoustic, pressure, IMU, GNSS, AIS).

 - The embedded software and autopilot stack, firmware, and the low-level glue that makes autonomy reliable in a brutal, wet, salty environment.

 - Robustness and efficiency engineering: watertight integrity, power budgets and endurance, thermal behaviour, EMC, and graceful failure modes far from shore.

 - Hands-on bring-up and sea trials — you'll be on the dock and on the boat, not just at the bench.

 - Vendor and component decisions, and the build-up of our test and manufacturing practices as we scale from prototype to fielded units.




THE HARD PROBLEMS YOU'LL BE SOLVING

These are genuinely difficult, and that's the point:

 - Persistence with a finite power budg

source_jobs.title: Step into the Hexa ecosystem
source_jobs.description:
JOIN OUR NETWORK OF EXCEPTIONAL TALENT

Be part of what's next at Hexa

We want to know more about you! But before that, a bit of context about us.


About Hexa

Hexa started as eFounders in 2011 to rethink company creation: building alongside founders, not from the sidelines. We've built over 50 companies, including Aircall, Spendesk, Front, Swan, and Yousign, with a combined valuation of over $5 billion, and companies employing more than 2,600 talented people all around the world.

Our hands-on model has proven successful. Now a leading multi-stage startup studio, we stay true to our DNA: founder-first and deeply involved, across AI, Future of Work, fintech, climate, defense and cybersecurity. Because when ambitious founders and teams come together, exceptional companies are born.

To fulfill this incredible purpose, we're constantly looking for exceptional individuals. Whether you're planning on launching a top tier company or joining one, here's a simple and quick get-to-know-you form that will help us match you with the best opportunities from the Hexa family.


Start a Company with us

Partner with us for 12 months to build the foundations of a new category-defining company — and 10x your chances of success with our team as your co-founder.



Join the Core Team

The team that co-builds every startup we launch. You work across multiple ventures in parallel, embedded with founders from day one, across product, design, go-to-market, recruiting, finance, and legal. If you want to be at the heart of company creation, this is it.



Join a Hexa venture early

Every year, Hexa co-founds around 12 new startups. Joining one in its first months means taking a founding employee role: you build the product, shape the culture, and own a real stake in the outcome, with the full support of Hexa's Core Team behind you.



Join a Hexa portfolio company

Some of our startups are further along. They're scaling, hiring, and looking for strong profiles to help them grow. If you want the energy of a startup with more structure and traction already in place, this is the path.



Your turn! :))

source_jobs.title: Expert Assurance | Courtage & IA @Panora (backed by Hexa)
source_jobs.description:
À PROPOS DE PANORA

Panora est une plateforme d'IA native pour les courtiers en assurance. Nos agents IA automatisent les workflows à forte friction, cotation, comparaison de contrats, analyse des garanties, vérification de conformité, génération de documents et produisent des résultats structurés et actionnables en quelques minutes plutôt qu'en heures.

Nous accompagnons les cabinets de courtage en France et en Belgique, avec une conviction claire : le courtage en assurance est opérationnellement dysfonctionnel depuis des décennies, et l'IA est enfin le bon outil pour y remédier. L'IA n'est plus optionnelle pour les courtiers : elle devient le standard. La question n'est plus si, mais à quel point elle est bien mise en œuvre.

Panora a été fondée par Diane et Fabian. Diane a passé plusieurs années en capital-risque avec une solide expérience opérationnelle et connaît de l'intérieur les défis du scaling de business complexes. Fabian est un fondateur multi-récidiviste et ingénieur IA orienté produit, pionnier dans la construction de systèmes IA qui fonctionnent vraiment en production. Ensemble, ils ont décidé de créer Panora pour transformer les opérations d'assurance en avantage concurrentiel en temps réel pour les courtiers.

Panora est soutenue par Hexa (anciennement eFounders), le studio de startups pionnier en Europe, qui a lancé plus de 35 entreprises dont des licornes comme Front, Aircall et Spendesk.




POURQUOI CE RÔLE EXISTE

Nous avons construit une IA qui lit des contrats, compare des garanties et génère des analyses de couverture à grande échelle. La technologie fonctionne. Nous avons maintenant besoin de quelqu'un qui la rende brillante : quelqu'un d'assez méticuleux pour détecter chaque lacune, d'assez expérimenté pour savoir ce qui compte vraiment dans une police, et d'assez curieux pour vouloir façonner l'IA de l'intérieur.

Vous serez le cerveau assurance de Panora, la personne qui examine ce que notre IA produit, vérifie chaque synthèse, et réinjecte son expertise dans le système pour qu'il s'améliore en continu. C'est une opportunité unique de vivre la transformation par l'IA de l'intérieur et de participer activement à la construction du futur du courtage.




VOS RESPONSABILITÉS

 - Vérification des outputs IA, contrat par contrat. Vous examinez les synthèses, analyses de couverture et recommandations générées par notre IA. Vous vérifiez leur exactitude, corrigez les erreurs et garantissez que chaque output répond au niveau d'exigence qu'un courtier engagerait sa réputation à défendre. Votre œil méticuleux est la dernière ligne de défense avant que les outputs n'atteignent les clients.

 - La boucle de feedback qui rend notre IA plus intelligente. Vous identifiez les schémas dans ce que l'IA réussit et rate. Vous travaillez avec les équipes produit et engineering pour réinjecter votre expertise assurance dans le système — améliorer les prompts, affiner le raisonnement, encoder les nuances que seules des années d'expérience permettent de saisir.

 - Accompagnement client et confiance. Vous soutenez l'onboarding des clients et leur usage au quotidien, en aidant les courtiers à comprendre et faire confiance aux outputs de l'IA. Quand des questions surgissent, vous apportez la crédibilité et l'expertise nécessaires pour expliquer, rassurer ou corriger.

 - Expertise assurance en IARD et Santé. Vous apportez une expertise approfondie en assurance dommages (IARD) et en assurance santé : les deux piliers de notre produit actuel. Vous en maîtrisez les spécificités et garantissez que notre IA traite les deux avec précision.




VOTRE PROFIL

 - Expérience en tant que courtier, souscripteur, ou dans un rôle d'analyse technique des couvertures

 - Expertise approfondie en IARD et/ou santé : vous connaissez ces produits sur le bout des doigts

 - Méticuleux de nature : vous êtes la personne qui repère l'exclusion enfouie page 47

 - Vous lisez un contrat et identifiez immédiatement ce qui compte, ce qui manque, et ce

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer @Panora (backed by Hexa)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PANORA

Panora is building a suite of AI-powered agents for insurance brokers - a highly regulated and operationally complex industry.

Our ambition is to become the AI Operating System for brokers in Europe: automating high-friction workflows while improving compliance, advisory quality, and client relationships.

The product is already live with brokerage firms across France and Belgium, with AI assistants in production (quotation automation, contract comparison, coverage analysis, compliance checks, document generation…).

Panora enables brokers to save hours every week, reduce analysis time by up to 70%, and focus on what truly matters: advising clients .

Founded by Diane du Paty (ex-VC, operator) and Fabian Langlet (repeat founder, AI product engineer), Panora is backed by Hexa (Aircall, Spendesk, Front).

We are entering a key phase of scaling and industrializing our AI platform.

At Panora, AI is not a feature - it is the core product layer, deployed in real-world conditions with strong constraints on reliability, traceability, and compliance.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Software Engineer, you'll join a small engineering team working directly with Fabian (CTO & Co-founder) and Jeremy (Founding Software Engineer).

You'll help build, improve and scale Panora's core product, turning customer problems into reliable features used every day by insurance brokers.

Your work will sit at the intersection of:

 - Backend engineering

 - AI-powered product features

 - Data processing and integrations

 - Product development

You'll have ownership over meaningful parts of the product while learning how to build robust software in a complex, regulated environment.




WHAT TO EXPECT

 - You'll join an existing product already used by real customers.

 - You'll spend as much time improving existing systems as building new features.

 - You'll work with complex workflows, unstructured data and production constraints.

 - You'll own features end-to-end, from implementation to deployment.

 - You'll work closely with users, founders and the rest of the engineering team to continuously improve the product.


RESPONSIBILITIES

Build Product

 - Design, build and ship product features used daily by insurance brokers.

 - Improve existing workflows and build new ones across the platform.

 - Work closely with the founders to turn customer feedback into product improvements.

Engineering

 - Build backend services, integrations and AI-powered workflows.

 - Write clean, maintainable and reliable production code.

 - Improve system quality, performance and observability as the product scales.

Grow with the Team

 - Take ownership of features from idea to production.

 - Contribute to architecture and engineering best practices.

 - Help shape how engineering evolves at Panora.
   
   


OUR TECHNOLOGY STACK

Our stack is designed to build reliable AI-powered products.

 - Backend: TypeScript, Node.js

 - Frontend: React, Next.js

 - Infrastructure: AWS (serverless & managed services)

 - AI & Data: LLMs, document processing, AI workflows

 - Integrations: Microsoft 365, CRM, insurer systems

You don't need experience with every technology. What matters most is your ability to learn quickly, write high-quality code and solve real customer problems.




WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We're looking for a builder who enjoys shipping products and working closely with a small, highly collaborative team.

 - You've already had a first experience building and shipping production software in a startup or product-driven environment.

 - You're comfortable with backend development (TypeScript/Node.js or equivalent) and enjoy solving real engineering problems.

 - You've worked with APIs, cloud infrastructure or data-intensive applications.

 - You're curious about AI and excited to build products powered by modern AI systems.

 - You're comfortable joining an existing codebase and improving it incrementally.

 - You care about building reliable products that

notes: Hexa is a startup studio with multiple portfolio companies, resulting in a diverse set of job postings.
